content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95814 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                              322-4441

February 18, 1976

A76-62-041

Mr. Ronald Kaldor
Executive Vice President
California Republican League
47 DeRose Court
Sacramento, Calif. 95833

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-015

Dear Mr. Kaldor:

Thank you for your letter of January 30, 1976,
requesting an opinion concerning the reporting require-
ments of the Menlo-Atherton Chapter of the California
Republican League (CRL). Your letter poses four questions
concerning political activities by the Chapter. This
letter is to advise you that the Commission will issue an
opinion in response to questions (a) and (d). Questions
(b) and (c) do not raise substantial questions involving
an interpretation of the Political Reform Act and, there-
fore, will be answered informally in this letter.

In response to questions (b) and (c), if the funds
collected to support receptions for particular candidates
and to make monetary and non-monetary contributions to
candidates equal or exceed $500, the Chapter is a committee.
Government Code Section 82013. However, volunteer personal
services are not "contributions," as that term is defined
in the Political Reform Act, and the value of such services
need not be counted in determining whether the requisite
$500 level is attained.

The Commission reserves the right to request more
information that the Commission or its staff considers rele-
vant to the preparation of the opinion. A draft opinion will
be prepared by the staff for presentation to the Commission,
and a copy of this draft will be furnished to you in approxi-
mately 60 days. Pending adoption of that opinion, the Menlo-

(2)

Mr. Ronald Kaltzer
February 18, 1976
Page Two

Atherton Chapter need not file campaign statements if its
activities consist only of those described in questions (a)
and (d).

If you have any questions, please contact Natalie
West, an attorney with the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:jp

47 DeRow Court
Sacramento, California 95833
January 30, 1976


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Chairman Lowenstein:

This letter is to request a legal opinion regarding the following
facts:

1. The California Republican League (CRL) is a statewide volun-
   teer political organization, the ultimate purpose of which
   is the election of Republican candidates to partisan office.

2. The CRL has local chapters located in various parts of the
   state which engage in activities affecting partisan and non-
   partisan activities.

3. The CRL files as a campaign committee and files appropriate
   campaign reports.

Question: (a) Must the Menlo-Atherton chapter of the CRL file
as a campaign committee if it receives over $500.00 in "dues"
and spends over $500.00 only on publications and meetings
informing and educating their members on the methods of electing
candidates from their particular political party?

(b) Does this local unit have to file a campaign committee state-
ment if, in addition to the above, it holds receptions honoring
particular candidates?

(c) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee
if, in addition to the above, it contributes money, volunteers,
or other campaign assistance to particular candidates?

(d) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee
if, in addition to the above, it receives funds and makes expen-
ditures for the specific purpose of conducting an event of the
parent CRL such as a state convention? Assume in this case that
funds received and spent for the convention were both over $500.00.

Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Page 2
January 30, 1976


In all of the above, assume that the local unit is acting
within the bylaws of the state political organization.

                                                    Yours truly,

                                                    [signature]

                                                    RONALD KALDOR
                                                    Executive Vice President
                                                    California Republican League

RK:js

sections.question:
(a) Must the Menlo-Atherton chapter of the CRL file as a campaign committee if it receives over $500.00 in "dues" and spends over $500.00 only on publications and meetings informing and educating their members on the methods of electing candidates from their particular political party?

(b) Does this local unit have to file a campaign committee statement if, in addition to the above, it holds receptions honoring particular candidates?

(c) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee if, in addition to the above, it contributes money, volunteers, or other campaign assistance to particular candidates?

(d) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee if, in addition to the above, it receives funds and makes expenditures for the specific purpose of conducting an event of the parent CRL such as a state convention? Assume in this case that funds received and spent for the convention were both over $500.00.

Mr. Daniel Lowenstein    
January 30, 1976

In all of the above, assume that the local unit is acting within the bylaws of the state political organization.

Yours truly,

RONALD KALDOR  
Executive Vice President  
California Republican League

(b) Does this local unit have to file a campaign committee statement if, in addition to the above, it holds receptions honoring particular candidates?

(c) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee if, in addition to the above, it contributes money, volunteers, or other campaign assistance to particular candidates?

(d) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee if, in addition to the above, it receives funds and makes expenditures for the specific purpose of conducting an event of the parent CRL such as a state convention? Assume in this case that funds received and spent for the convention were both over $500.00.

Mr. Daniel Lowenstein    
January 30, 1976

In all of the above, assume that the local unit is acting within the bylaws of the state political organization.

Yours truly,

RONALD KALDOR  
Executive Vice President  
California Republican League
